cards.back: The practice of providing clear and understandable information about the data, models, and decision-making processes used in AI systems
The responsibility of developers, deployers, and users to ensure that AI systems are designed, deployed, and used in ways that respect human rights and adhere to laws and regulations
The principle that AI systems should not discriminate against or disadvantage certain groups of people, and should make decisions that are impartial and unbiased
The ability of AI systems to provide clear and understandable explanations for their decisions and actions, so that users can understand how they arrive at their conclusions
The practice of ensuring that sensitive personal data is collected, stored, and used in ways that respect individuals' privacy and security
